title: forsteronia subcordata
intro: Forsteronia subcordata, also known as the 'Pituri' or 'Pituri vine', is a plant native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Australia and New Caledonia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Forsteronia subcordata include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as potential applications in treating various skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
